channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: On Arm PC return rates and CEO posturing
item.description: With her claim that retailers are seeing high returns of Arm PCs, Intel interim CEO Michelle Johnston Holthaus appears to be trying to scare buyers off the rival processor architecture. But enterprise buyers who look before they leap have little to fear.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 6 ways AI can help UX designers work better—and faster
item.description: Artificial intelligence isn’t creative in the same way that people are. It doesn’t understand inspiration. But that doesn’t mean real UX designers can’t use it. AI can help a designer be more efficient and creative, and do a few more things that might be surprising.
item.dc:creator: Richard Baguley, Fast Company

item.title: 19 percent in new survey have invested in, traded or used cryptocurrency
item.description: About 1-in-5 voters have invested in, traded or used cryptocurrency, according to a Tuesday poll. The survey from Emerson College Polling found that 19 percent of respondents have traded, invested or used crypto like Bitcoin or Ethereum. About 81 percent have not.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: It’s the end of the internet as we know it—and I feel fine
item.description: The internet feels like it’s falling apart. Not literally. Structurally, it’s sound. There are plenty of fiber optic cables lining ocean floors, cell towers looming above cityscapes, and server-filled data centers.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: Nima Momeni Found Guilty in Bob Lee Stabbing Death
item.description: Nima Momeni convicted of second-degree murder in the stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Want to ditch Microsoft Teams? Skype is still here, and just made a significant change
item.description: You can no longer buy credits on Skype as Microsoft pushes the platform to become subscription-based.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Operational silos could overwhelm more enterprises in the future
item.description: Operational silos across NetOps, SecOps and CloudOps teams could lead to critical security vulnerabilities.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The future of business processes: Three functions that GenAI will transform
item.description: It’s time for organizations to move beyond the experimentation phase and tap into tangible use-cases.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Marvel Rivals Gets Limited-Time Game Mode and Winter Skins - December Update Revealed
item.description: NetEase announced a limited-time game mode, Jeff's Winter Splash Festival, for Marvel Rivals this week. The 4v4 Splatoon-like mode releases on December 20 along with themed skins for Marvel heroes.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: EU investigates TikTok over Romanian presidential election safeguards
item.description: LONDON (AP) — European Union regulators said Tuesday they're investigating whether TikTok breached the bloc's digital rulebook by failing to deal with risks to Romania's presidential election, which has been thrown into turmoil over allegations of electoral violations and Russian meddling.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Avnet CIO: Navigating the cloud and AI landscape with a practical approach
item.description: As CIO of Avnet — one of the largest technology distributors and supply chain solution providers — I’m responsible for the organization’s IT stack and oversee digital transformation and strategy. Two critical areas that underpin our digital approach are cloud and artificial intelligence (AI).
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 5 reasons why 2025 will be the year of OpenTelemetry
item.description: Some open-source projects are spectacularly successful and become standard components of the IT infrastructure. Take Linux or Kubernetes, for example.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 10 steps to turn a failed project into an opportunity
item.description: What got you here will keep getting you here — unless you stop to figure out why it’s happening in the first place. When a project goes off the rails, it’s tempting to focus on the urgent: fix the immediate issues to get things back on track.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: This Mark Cuban-backed startup is helping creators monetize their audiences
item.description: Falon Fatemi first met Mark Cuban in 2011 at South By Southwest, where the two bonded over ideas about untapped opportunities in the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: Janya Sundar, Fast Company

item.title: Senate report alleges Amazon rejected warehouse safety recommendations due to productivity concerns
item.description: At least two internal Amazon studies found a link between how quickly the online retailer's warehouse workers perform tasks and workplace injuries, but the company rejected many safety recommendations out of concern the proposed changes might reduce productivity, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to upgrade to the Hyte Y70 PC case to give your parts more breathing room
item.description: The Hyte Y70 is a stylish PC case that will help PC gamers get the most out of their graphics cards and other high-end parts
item.dc:creator: Gieson Cacho, The Mercury News

item.title: Why Thrive Capital’s Vince Hankes is betting big on OpenAI
item.description: “We’re in this massive paradigm shift,” says Vince Hankes, the venture capitalist leading Thrive Capital’s investment in OpenAI. “And when that happens, historically, there have been new companies created that are worth a lot of money.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Your phone got hacked and now someone's got control of it. What to do immediately
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson lays out the immediate steps you should take if your phone has been hacked and your personal information becomes vulnerable.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Court denies TikTok's request to halt enforcement of potential US ban until Supreme Court review
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday left in place a mid-January deadline in a federal law requiring TikTok to be sold or face a ban in the United States, rejecting a request made by the company to halt enforcement until the Supreme Court reviews its challenge of the statute.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Sophia, a famous robot and global icon of AI, wins hearts at Zimbabwe's innovation fair
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — From answering questions from Cabinet ministers, academics and students on climate change, substance abuse and the law to children’s inquiries about her “birth” and links to God and being described as a talkative feminist, Sophia, the world-famous robot won hearts at an innov
item.dc:creator: BY FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Heading north for the winter? Prepare your EV with these tips
item.description: New research from AAA reveals that when temperatures dip to 20 degrees Fahrenheit, and the HVAC system is used to heat the inside of the vehicle, the average driving range of an EV decreases by 41 percent.
item.dc:creator: Mark Maynard, The Mercury News

item.title: Federal judge slaps down Automattic, granting temporary injunction to WP Engine in ongoing WordPress squabble
item.description: The battle between WordPress owner Automattic and WP Engine seemingly struck US federal Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin as rather one-sided, as she ruled against Automattic on Tuesday and granted WP Engine the preliminary injunction it sought.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
